summary: Technical success during hepatic arterial embolization—including accurate catheter placement, effective drug/particle delivery, and achievement of vascular stasis—is essential for targeting liver tumors properly. This impacts treatment efficacy, enables potential curative interventions such as resection later, and correlates with improved survival rates among patients undergoing this therapy.
